observations: Opening pass: kept Sylvanas in clipped field judgment by changing 'Naming it would only tighten the air between them' to the more physical 'Naming it would only make her stiffen.'
Road-memory pass: replaced 'He had line on what was left to learn' with 'He had seen enough' so Cyndia's line stayed scout-plain instead of sounding writerly.
Camp-register pass: changed 'the whole place assumed a visible war' to 'the whole place had been built around a visible war' to keep Sylvanas in concrete assessment rather than abstract summary.
Political-language pass: cut the arc-summary phrasing in the dispatch section and replaced it with 'No more pretending she was filing border reports from safe ground.'
Closing pass: replaced 'a flaw big enough to swallow cities' with 'the kind of certainty Uther had learned to fear' so the final paragraph stayed inside what Sylvanas has actually read on-page.
examples: Cyndia grief beat: abstract atmospheric phrasing compressed into bodily reaction.
Checkpoint dialogue: Falric and Sylvanas kept in crisp command language, with no decorative flourish added.
Command-tent narration: Arthas and Uther introduced through posture, map use, and authority signals instead of explanatory biography.
Dispatch paragraph: clipped dossier rhythm softened into scene-bound consequence.
Final lakeward push: geography kept tactical and immediate rather than thematic.

observations: Checked the opening countryside paragraph: every detail now arrives through what Sylvanas can smell, touch, count, or judge in the field.
Checked Falric at the checkpoint: 'Something moved behind Falric's eyes' stays on visible reaction rather than claiming his interior thought.
Checked the Arthas briefing scene: Sylvanas reads his speed, his hand on the map, and the officers leaning toward him; the prose does not step outside her to certify motive.
Checked Uther exchange outside the tent: kept the read on hands, mouth, jaw, and weariness rather than translating his entire grief into narrator verdict.
Checked the final relay pivot: Jaina remains off-page as absence, report, and risk only; the chapter never claims access to what happened below Caer Darrow.
examples: Removed implied magic-certainty in 'Jaina would have read the wards cut into those orchard rows' and grounded it as Sylvanas noticing that the rows were wrong.
Trimmed border-fiction abstraction into a lived field realization.
Kept Cyndia's doubled check physical, not psychological.
Kept Arthas's danger in observed certainty rather than prophetic narration.
Kept the Darrowmere relay strictly external: entry, crossing, no return.

notes: Cyndia/Lyriel continuity holds: the doubled right-flank check remains the live consequence of the earlier loss, and Sylvanas notices it without overexplaining it.
Eastweald continuity holds: productive farm country still wears order as camouflage, matching chapter10's Barov-line read rather than turning suddenly into ruin-country.
Arthas/Sylvanas continuity holds: he remains mutually legible but not aligned with her, a necessary instrument rather than a trusted ally.
Lordaeron command-refusal continuity holds: the problem is structural lateness, not simple disbelief, and the dispatch section preserves that pressure.
Jaina/Sylvanas continuity holds across the chapter break: Sylvanas misses Jaina first as missing field judgment and magical read, not as sentimental absence.
observations: Verified names and titles: Ranger-General Sylvanas Windrunner, Prince Arthas, Captain Falric, and Uther remain consistent with prior files.
Verified geography: southbound to Hearthglen, northbound back toward Darrowmere and Caer Darrow.
Verified injuries: Sylvanas's wound is healing, not gone.
Verified partnership state: split threads stay simultaneous.
Verified unresolved thread handling: no Mal'Ganis resolution or old-farewell payoff intrudes here.
Broke the checkpoint inventory monotony by keeping the camp read in varied sentence lengths instead of a repeated 'The [noun] [verb]' chain.
Kept short-sentence pressure in the opening road paragraphs but punctured it with one fuller sentence at the right moments so the beat did not go machine-flat.
Smoothed the command-tent explanation run by shortening the mechanism language and letting questions and map moves break the cadence.
Varied the dispatch section so the relay does not arrive as four identical clipped fragments in a row.
Kept the ending paragraphs alternating between blunt movement sentences and one longer sentence of consequence, which gives the last gallop room to land.
examples: Opening: 'This should have been cart country. Harness noise. Wheels sunk...' remains a deliberate pressure run, not accidental monotony.
Camp read: sentence lengths now move from fragment to compound to fragment.
Briefing section: Arthas's short lines cut against Sylvanas's fuller intelligence delivery.
Uther scene: medium-length reflection broken by single-line dialogue answers.
Close: the final three paragraphs alternate observation, judgment, and action cleanly.

continuity.crossChapter: Verified against chapter10: chapter11 opens on absence, split-thread pressure, and missing magical read rather than recapping Jaina's descent.
observations: Scene 1 road work carries causally into the camp arrival: the land-scale evidence forces the institutional handoff.
Checkpoint friction now turns directly into tent access without dead air or temporal filler.
The briefing converts into mobilization through Arthas's map decisions, not by summary time-skip.
The dispatch section earns the northward redirect by stacking recall pressure before the Jaina emergency arrives.
The last gallop out of camp preserves the chapter bridge: this is the decision that creates chapter13's arrival, while chapter12 stays in the simultaneous interior lane.
examples: The Hearthglen camp is visibly built for open war, which causally sets up Sylvanas's distrust of the coming mobilization.
Uther's warning sits between briefing and march, separating two similar success beats so they do not blur.
Lor'themar's dispatch changes the strategic read before the Darrowmere rider changes the personal priority.
The final 'North' exchange acts as action trigger, not chapter-summary ornament.
No scene break marker was introduced where the prose wanted continuous momentum.

observations: Cut lingering planner-style abstraction from the dispatch paragraph and final coda.
Removed the visible bold formatting on Lor'themar's line so the prose has to earn emphasis by cadence alone.
Trimmed one overreaching prophetic line at the close into a present-tense tactical fear.
Kept scene openings free of throat-clearing weather clichés; every opening paragraph now starts in motion or assessment.
Re-read the weakest sections—the mechanism explanation, dispatch paragraph, and final lakeward paragraph—and tightened each toward concrete field language.
examples: Dispatch emphasis now comes from the line itself, not markup.
The last paragraph no longer sermonizes about the chapter's theme.
The Jaina-absence beats stay operational, not explanatory.
The camp inventory remains story pressure, not worldbuilding tour.
No biography clause was reintroduced around Uther, Falric, or Arthas.

observations: The main contamination issue was Sylvanas's line 'This teaches the body first and uses death second,' which sounded too much like a paper abstract.
Replaced it with 'This travels first. Then the dead get up,' keeping the same warning in speech a field commander would actually use.
Checked Arthas's lines: command register stays human and military, not bureaucratic.
Checked Uther's lines: moral gravity stays plainspoken and spoken aloud, never report-like.
Checked Cyndia and Falric: both remain concise service-professional voices without institutional sludge.
examples: Sylvanas now briefs consequence and spread, not theory language.
Arthas's 'No bread from local stores...' reads as direct command, not memo prose.
Uther's 'Not enough to stop thinking' stays sermon-sharp but natural in speech.
Falric's gatekeeping remains chain-of-command formal, not bureaucratese.
Cyndia's 'North' lands as field shorthand, exactly where it should.
